attachment.content:
- frontend-design
- r-audit: Run an external audit of a pqverse R package (MiscMetabar, tidypq, comparpq). Creates independent test data, runs all exported functions, and produces an R script and a TODO report of warnings, errors, and recommendations. Use this skill to stress-test a package with edge cases (NAs, empty samples, zero-count taxa, malformed trees).
- r-build: Build the pkgdown documentation website and the R package source tarball. Use this skill whenever the user wants to build their R package, rebuild the pkgdown site, regenerate documentation, build the README, lint the package.
- r-check: Style, document, and run R CMD check on the current R package, then propose fixes for any issues found. Use this skill whenever the user wants to check, validate, or verify their R package, run R CMD check, fix NOTEs/WARNINGs/ERRORs.
- r-pkg-bump-version: Bump an R package from a development version (X.Y.Z.9000) to a release version and finalize the NEWS.md heading. Use this skill whenever the user wants to bump the version of an R package for release, prepare DESCRIPTION + NEWS.md for CRAN, finalize the version before merging dev → master, or trigger Stage 8 of the release pipeline.
- r-pkg-merge-release: Merge an R package's dev branch into master, push, and bump dev to the next development cycle (X.Y.Z → X.Y.Z.9000). Use this skill after Stage 8 (version bump) when the user wants to finalize the release, merge dev into master, push to origin, start the next dev cycle, or trigger Stage 9 of the release pipeline.
- r-pkg-release: Full release pipeline for R packages: runs format, document, R CMD check (--as-cran), tests, optional coverage/audit/win-builder/rhub, CRAN extra checks, and builds the package. Supports --auto mode for autonomous runs that only stop for true ambiguity, with a resumable state file. Use this skill whenever the user wants to release a package, prepare for CRAN submission, run a pre-release check, do a full validation pipeline, or get a package ready for release. Also trigger when the user mentions 'release checklist', 'CRAN ready', 'prepare release'.
- r-stats
- r-test: Run the test suite and code coverage for the current R package, then propose new tests to improve coverage and fix failing tests. Use this skill whenever the user wants to run tests, check test coverage, fix failing tests, submit to Codecov.
- r-viz
- web-design-guidelines
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- posit-dev:critical-code-reviewer
- posit-dev:describe-design
- open-source:release-post
- open-source:create-release-checklist
- r-lib:testing-r-packages
- r-lib:cli
- r-lib:cran-extrachecks: Prepare R packages for CRAN submission by checking for common ad-hoc requirements not caught by devtools::check(). Use when: (1) Preparing a package for first CRAN release, (2) Preparing a package update for CRAN resubmission, (3) Reviewing a package to ensure CRAN compliance, (4) Responding to CRAN reviewer feedback. Covers documentation requirements, DESCRIPTION field standards, URL validation, examples, and administrative requirements.
- r-lib:lifecycle: Guidance for managing R package lifecycle according to tidyverse principles using the lifecycle package. Use when: (1) Setting up lifecycle infrastructure in a package, (2) Deprecating functions or arguments, (3) Renaming functions or arguments, (4) Superseding functions, (5) Marking functions as experimental, (6) Understanding lifecycle stages (stable, experimental, deprecated, superseded), or (7) Writing deprecation helpers for complex scenarios.
- r-lib:r-package-development
- r-lib:r-cli-app
- r-lib:mirai
- r-lib:alt-text
- quarto:brand-yml
- quarto:quarto-authoring
- quarto:alt-text
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.content:
1	# pqverse ROADMAP
2	
3	## How to read this file
4	
5	Each item is tagged with a **priority** and a **facility** level:
6	
7	| Priority | Meaning |
8	|---|---|
9	| **Critical** | Release-blocking; breaks documented workflows |
10	| **High** | Next-release target; prototype or clear design already exists |
11	| **Medium** | Planned; requires design work or new dependency |
12	| **Low** | Nice-to-have, deferred, or long-term vision |
13	
14	| Facility | Meaning |
15	|---|---|
16	| **easy** | Self-contained, < ~150 LOC, no new dependency |
17	| **moderate** | A few functions + tests, new CRAN dependency, or dataset bundling |
18	| **hard** | New S4/S7 class, cross-package change, upstream permission needed, or non-CRAN dependency |
19	
20	Format: `**[Priority / facility]**` — description. `(source: path[:lines])`
21	
22	> **Open decision** (see bottom of file): Windows support posture.
23	
24	---
25	
26	# MiscMetabar
27	
28	## Architecture
29	
30	- **[moderate / hard]** New `extended_phyloseq` S4 class with a `tax_data` data.frame slot supporting numeric and list columns for per-taxon traits (solves the two limitations of `tax_table`: no numeric columns, no list columns). Or just a tiny function that transform a tax_table into a data.frame with numeric and list columns. 
31	
32	## Data I/O
33	
34	- **[High / easy]** `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` — export OTU table, sample data, taxonomy, and refseq to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF MDT submission. Add Darwin Core DNA-derived-data extension compliance checks; optionally use `tidypq` for pre-export modifications.
35	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/phyloseq_to_MDT_excel.R`; `ROADMAP.md`:57–116)
36	
37	- **[High / easy]** `clean_pq()` gains a NA-cleanup option: replace `"NA"` and `"NA NA"` strings with true `<NA>` values (default `FALSE` to avoid breaking changes).
38	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:16–21)
39	
40	- **[Medium / easy]** `vs_cluster_subseq()` — expose `--query_cov` and `--target_cov` as direct arguments (currently only settable via `vsearch_options`).
41	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:52–62)
42	
43	- **[Medium / easy]** Align `write.csv` output to the GBIF MDT CSV template format (distinct from the Excel exporter already planned).
44	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme; https://docs.gbif-uat.org/mdt-user-guide/en/#fitting-the-data-into-a-template)
45	
46	## Analysis / Statistics
47	
48	- **[High / hard]** : Continue to implement bootstrapping (functions with rarperm in their name) by looking at https://f1000research.com/articles/14-87/v1 . May be we should create a new package for this in the pqverse. Is it possible to use one master function for all bootstrapping functions? See https://journals.asm.org/doi/10.1128/msphere.00354-23 for a theoretical overview of bootstrapping. Note that I will prefer use bootstrapping word in place of rarefaction because it is often confused with rarefy. rarefy_even_depth_pq is not rarefaction (=bootstrapping) but rarefying (https://forum.qiime2.org/t/rarefaction-vs-rarefying-and-when-to-use-them-in-your-analysis-pipeline/33251). Note that bootstrapping (rarefaction) use rarefying methodology.
49	
50	- **[High / moderate]** Incorporate ecodive R package (https://github.com/cmmr/ecodive) to compute beta diversity metrics, in particular robust Aitchison distances (https://cmmr.github.io/ecodive/reference/robust_aitchison.html). It must be far more quick than vegan implementations. First, create a short benchmark to compare the two implementations. Second, if the benchmark is satisfying list the functions that used betadiversity metrics and third- implement the ecodive functions in the `diversity_pq()` wrapper.
51	
52	
53	- **[High / moderate]** TINA and PINA computation via `tina_pq()` and `pina_pq()`. We must use two repos (https://github.com/defleury/Schmidt_et_al_2016_community_similarity/blob/8ef50591278925a2384324d4a56b0b633dd91104/functions.community_similarity.R for function community.similarity.corr.par and https://github.com/trtcrd/Bench_sedaDNA/blob/53962761cf890a8e3f817f8371b27e2977410c72/Collect_stats_synthetic_datasets_clusters.R especially lines 583 to 601) and two papers (https://doi.org/10.1038/ismej.2016.139, 10.1093/ismeco/ycaf230).
54	
55	- **[High / moderate]** `aldex_pq()` wrapper with three test modes (kw, t, corr) — full prototype exists; inspired by EasyMicrobiomeR. Add as standalone function and vignette.
56	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:83–203)
57	
58	- **[Medium / moderate]** `check_distance()` adapted from Betadiag — checks distance matrix validity before PCoA / PERMANOVA / MiRKAT. Add `check_dist = TRUE` default to all distance-computing functions with `cli::warn()` feedback.
59	  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:52–54; https://github.com/bioscinema/Betadiag)
60	
61	- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary plot for biomarker taxa using `lefser` + `ggtern`.
62	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1106–1179)
63	
64	- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary + diamond plots for 3–4 group compositions — port Mahendra Mariadassou's implementation, simplify with `psmelt`. **Requires permission from M. Mariadassou before use.**
65	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1182–1525)
66	
67	- **[Medium / moderate]** Correlation iconography — wrappers around `HelpersMG::IC_threshold_matrix()` and `IC_correlation_simplify()`.
68	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:955–1005)
69	
70	- **[Medium / moderate]** GDM (Generalized Dissimilarity Modelling) wrapper using `gdm::formatsitepair()` + `gdm.varImp()`.
71	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1938–2004)
72	
73	- **[Medium / moderate]** TITAN2 threshold indicator analysis wrapper.
74	  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2031–2051)

79	- **[Low / moderate]** Hill accumulation with `divent` (replaces iNEXT approach) + proper singleton handling. **Action required: discuss with É. Marcon.**

85	- **[Medium / easy]** `assign_sintax()` gains a vote-based consensus method alongside / instead of the `lcaout` output (e.g. majority-vote at configurable threshold).

92	  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme; https://cassandrahjo.github.io/Rsearch/)

file.content:
# pqverse ROADMAP

## How to read this file

Each item is tagged with a **priority** and a **facility** level:

| Priority | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Critical** | Release-blocking; breaks documented workflows |
| **High** | Next-release target; prototype or clear design already exists |
| **Medium** | Planned; requires design work or new dependency |
| **Low** | Nice-to-have, deferred, or long-term vision |

| Facility | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **easy** | Self-contained, < ~150 LOC, no new dependency |
| **moderate** | A few functions + tests, new CRAN dependency, or dataset bundling |
| **hard** | New S4/S7 class, cross-package change, upstream permission needed, or non-CRAN dependency |

Format: `**[Priority / facility]**` — description. `(source: path[:lines])`

> **Open decision** (see bottom of file): Windows support posture.

---

# MiscMetabar

## Architecture

- **[moderate / hard]** New `extended_phyloseq` S4 class with a `tax_data` data.frame slot supporting numeric and list columns for per-taxon traits (solves the two limitations of `tax_table`: no numeric columns, no list columns). Or just a tiny function that transform a tax_table into a data.frame with numeric and list columns. 

## Data I/O

- **[High / easy]** `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` — export OTU table, sample data, taxonomy, and refseq to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF MDT submission. Add Darwin Core DNA-derived-data extension compliance checks; optionally use `tidypq` for pre-export modifications.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/phyloseq_to_MDT_excel.R`; `ROADMAP.md`:57–116)

- **[High / easy]** `clean_pq()` gains a NA-cleanup option: replace `"NA"` and `"NA NA"` strings with true `<NA>` values (default `FALSE` to avoid breaking changes).
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:16–21)

- **[Medium / easy]** `vs_cluster_subseq()` — expose `--query_cov` and `--target_cov` as direct arguments (currently only settable via `vsearch_options`).
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:52–62)

- **[Medium / easy]** Align `write.csv` output to the GBIF MDT CSV template format (distinct from the Excel exporter already planned).
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, long terme; https://docs.gbif-uat.org/mdt-user-guide/en/#fitting-the-data-into-a-template)

## Analysis / Statistics

- **[High / hard]** : Continue to implement bootstrapping (functions with rarperm in their name) by looking at https://f1000research.com/articles/14-87/v1 . May be we should create a new package for this in the pqverse. Is it possible to use one master function for all bootstrapping functions? See https://journals.asm.org/doi/10.1128/msphere.00354-23 for a theoretical overview of bootstrapping. Note that I will prefer use bootstrapping word in place of rarefaction because it is often confused with rarefy. rarefy_even_depth_pq is not rarefaction (=bootstrapping) but rarefying (https://forum.qiime2.org/t/rarefaction-vs-rarefying-and-when-to-use-them-in-your-analysis-pipeline/33251). Note that bootstrapping (rarefaction) use rarefying methodology.

- **[High / moderate]** Incorporate ecodive R package (https://github.com/cmmr/ecodive) to compute beta diversity metrics, in particular robust Aitchison distances (https://cmmr.github.io/ecodive/reference/robust_aitchison.html). It must be far more quick than vegan implementations. First, create a short benchmark to compare the two implementations. Second, if the benchmark is satisfying list the functions that used betadiversity metrics and third- implement the ecodive functions in the `diversity_pq()` wrapper.


- **[High / moderate]** TINA and PINA computation via `tina_pq()` and `pina_pq()`. We must use two repos (https://github.com/defleury/Schmidt_et_al_2016_community_similarity/blob/8ef50591278925a2384324d4a56b0b633dd91104/functions.community_similarity.R for function community.similarity.corr.par and https://github.com/trtcrd/Bench_sedaDNA/blob/53962761cf890a8e3f817f8371b27e2977410c72/Collect_stats_synthetic_datasets_clusters.R especially lines 583 to 601) and two papers (https://doi.org/10.1038/ismej.2016.139, 10.1093/ismeco/ycaf230).

- **[High / moderate]** `aldex_pq()` wrapper with three test modes (kw, t, corr) — full prototype exists; inspired by EasyMicrobiomeR. Add as standalone function and vignette.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:83–203)

- **[Medium / moderate]** `check_distance()` adapted from Betadiag — checks distance matrix validity before PCoA / PERMANOVA / MiRKAT. Add `check_dist = TRUE` default to all distance-computing functions with `cli::warn()` feedback.
  (source: `ROADMAP.md`:52–54; https://github.com/bioscinema/Betadiag)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary plot for biomarker taxa using `lefser` + `ggtern`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1106–1179)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Ternary + diamond plots for 3–4 group compositions — port Mahendra Mariadassou's implementation, simplify with `psmelt`. **Requires permission from M. Mariadassou before use.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1182–1525)

- **[Medium / moderate]** Correlation iconography — wrappers around `HelpersMG::IC_threshold_matrix()` and `IC_correlation_simplify()`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:955–1005)

- **[Medium / moderate]** GDM (Generalized Dissimilarity Modelling) wrapper using `gdm::formatsitepair()` + `gdm.varImp()`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1938–2004)

- **[Medium / moderate]** TITAN2 threshold indicator analysis wrapper.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2031–2051)

- **[Medium / hard]** PLS-DA / sPLS-DA via `mixOmics`.
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:1011–1089)

- **[Low / moderate]** Hill accumulation with `divent` (replaces iNEXT approach) + proper singleton handling. **Action required: discuss with É. Marcon.**
  (source: `arround_MiscMetabar/en_cours_de_dev.qmd`:2134–2143)

- **[High / moderate]** Harmonize parameter names across all functions: `fact`, `bifactor`, `by.fact`, `merge_sample_by`, `modality`, `variable`, `var_to_test` — in progress.
  (source: `MiscMetabar/MiscMetabar.md`, court terme)

- **[Medium / easy]** `assign_sintax()` gains a vote-based consensus method alongside / instead of the `lcaout` output (e.g. majority-vote at configurable threshold).

# Proposed new packages

## netaipq — Networks and ML for phyloseq

The next new package to develop in the pqverse. Wraps network inference,
bipartite visualisation, and machine-learning methods for phyloseq objects.

# Cross-cutting concerns

- **Hill diversity convention** — all new Hill-based functions must use `divent::div_hill()` with `estimator = "UnveilJ"` as default (migration completed in MiscMetabar 0.15.1 and comparpq 0.2.1; deprecate `scales`/`hill_scales` params in favour of `q`).

- **Windows platform** — 13+ MiscMetabar functions are documented as non-working on Windows (`build_phytree_pq`, `cutadapt_remove_primers`, `krona`, `venn_pq`, etc.). Consider improving Docker workflow documentation as the primary mitigation.

- **Pending external contacts:**
  - **Mahendra Mariadassou** — permission to port ternary/diamond plot implementation into MiscMetabar.
  - **Éric Marcon** — discuss `divent`-based Hill accumulation and singleton handling approach.

- **Dataset licensing** — before bundling FungalTraits, TAXREFv18, or BDC_18 into any `inst/extdata`, verify that the respective licenses permit redistribution inside an R package.

# CRAN Submission Roadmap

> Goal: submit every pqverse package to CRAN, in dependency-first order.

## Submission order

The order is dictated by the dependency graph. Each package must be on CRAN before downstream dependents can submit.

| # | Package | Version | Bioconptron in Depends/Imports? | Non-CRAN Suggests? | Blockers | Readiness |
|---|---------|---------|------------------------------|-------------------|----------|-----------|
| 1 | greenAlgoR | 0.1.2 | No | No | Minor | High |
| 2 | tidypq | 0.1.1 | phyloseq (Depends) | Biostrings, dada2 | phyloseq risk | Medium-High |
| 3 | taxinfo | 0.1.2 | phyloseq (Imports) | gbif.range | LazyData, gbif.range guard | Medium |
| 4 | comparpq | 0.1.3 | phyloseq (Depends), Biostrings (Imports) | Multiple Bioc | Quarto vignette, Biostrings in Imports | Medium-Low |
| 5 | dbpq | 0.0.0.9000 | Biostrings (Imports) | dada2 | Version bump, Biostrings → Suggests | Low |
| 6 | MiscMetabar | 0.16.1.9000 | phyloseq, dada2 (Depends) | None critical | Already on CRAN; .html vignette cleanup | On CRAN |
| 7 | pqverse | 0.1.0 | None direct | None | ALL Imports not on CRAN yet | Very Low |

> MiscMetabar is already on CRAN and is listed last because it needs only maintenance fixes. pqverse must wait for every other package to clear CRAN first.

# Load necessary libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(openxlsx)

# Function to save Excel workbook
phyloseq_to_MDT_excel <- function(physeq, filename = "Phyloseq_Tables.xlsx") {
  # Create a new Excel workbook
  wb <- createWorkbook()
  
  # Save OTU Table
  if(taxa_are_rows(physeq)) {
    otu_table_df <- t(data.frame(otu_table(physeq)))
    } else {
    otu_table_df <- data.frame(otu_table(physeq))
  }
  addWorksheet(wb, "OTU_table")
  writeData(wb, "OTU_table", cbind(id = rownames(otu_table_df), otu_table_df))
  
  # Save Sample Data
  sample_data_df <- data.frame(sample_data(physeq))
  addWorksheet(wb, "Samples")
  writeData(wb, "Samples", cbind(id = rownames(sample_data_df), sample_data_df))
  
  # Save Taxonomy Table
  tax_table_df <- data.frame(tax_table(physeq))
  if(!is.null(refseq(physeq))){
    tax_table_df[,"DNA_sequence"] <- as.character(refseq(physeq))
  }
  addWorksheet(wb, "Taxonomy")
  writeData(wb, "Taxonomy", cbind(id = rownames(tax_table_df), tax_table_df))
  
  # Save the workbook
  saveWorkbook(wb, filename, overwrite = TRUE)
}



# Example usage
# Replace 'your_phyloseq_object' with your actual phyloseq object

library(MiscMetabar)

phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(clean_pq(data_fungi))

phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(clean_pq(data_fungi, force_taxa_as_rows = T), "eui.xlsx")

#' Fastp: Adaptater detection, quality control and preprocessing of FASTQ files 
#' 
#' @description
#' This function is a wrapper for fastp software to perform quality control
#' and preprocessing of FASTQ files.
#' 
#' To install fastp on linux: 
#' 
#' ```bash
#' wget http://opengene.org/fastp/fastp
#' chmod a+x ./fastp
#' sudo mv fastp /usr/local/bin/
#' ```
#' 
#' For other OS or specific installation instructions, please refer to the official fastp GitHub repository:
#' https://github.com/OpenGene/fastp 
#' 
#' @param path_to_fastq Path to the directory containing FASTQ files
#' @param folder_output Name of the output folder (default: "fastp_output")
#' @param nproc Number of threads to use (default: 1)
#' @param pattern Pattern to match FASTQ files (default: "fastq.gz")
#' @param pattern_R1 Pattern to identify R1 files (default: "_R1")
#' @param pattern_R2 Pattern to identify R2 files (default: "_R2")
#' @param nb_files Number of files to process (default: Inf for all files)
#' @param paired_end Logical, whether files are paired-end (default: TRUE)
#' @param cmd_is_run Logical, whether to run the command (default: TRUE)
#' @param return_file_path Logical, whether to return file paths (default: FALSE)
#' @param qualified_quality_phred Minimum base quality (default: 20)
#' @param average_qual Minimum average read quality (default: 20)
#' @param length_required Minimum read length after trimming (default: 50)
#' @param detect_adapter Logical, enable automatic adapter detection for PE (default: TRUE)
#' @param trim_poly_g Logical, trim poly-G tails (default: TRUE)
#' @param correction Logical, correct mismatches in overlapped PE reads (default: TRUE)
#' @param cut_front Logical, trim low quality bases from 5' end (default: TRUE)
#' @param cut_tail Logical, trim low quality bases from 3' end (default: TRUE)
#' @param cut_window_size Window size for sliding quality filter (default: 4)
#' @param cut_mean_quality Minimum mean quality for trimming window (default: 20)
#' @param extra_fastp_args Additional fastp arguments as a character string (default: "")
#'
#' @return If return_file_path is TRUE, returns list of output file paths
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' # Paired-end example
#' 
#' fastp(
#'   path_to_fastq = system.file("/extdata", package = "MiscMetabar"),
#'   folder_output = "qc_data",
#'   nproc = 8,
#'   paired_end = TRUE
#' )
#'
#' # Single-end example with extra options
#' fastp(
#'   path_to_fastq = "raw_data/",
#'   folder_output = "qc_data",
#'   nproc = 8,
#'   paired_end = FALSE,
#'   extra_fastp_args = "--merge --n_base_limit 5 --dedup"
#' )
#' }
#'
#' @author Adrien Taudière
#' @details
#' This function is a wrapper for the fastp software, please cite 
#'  fastp if you use this function.
#'

fastp <- function(path_to_fastq,
                  folder_output = "fastp_output",
                  nproc = 1,
                  pattern = "fastq.gz",
                  pattern_R1 = "_R1",
                  pattern_R2 = "_R2",
                  nb_files = Inf,
                  paired_end = TRUE,
                  cmd_is_run = TRUE,
                  return_file_path = FALSE,
                  qualified_quality_phred = 20,
                  average_qual = 20,
                  length_required = 50,
                  detect_adapter = TRUE,
                  trim_poly_g = TRUE,
                  correction = TRUE,
                  cut_front = TRUE,
                  cut_tail = TRUE,
                  cut_window_size = 4,
                  cut_mean_quality = 20,
                  extra_fastp_args = "") {
  
  # Create output folder if it doesn't exist
  if (!dir.exists(folder_output)) {
    dir.create(folder_output)
  }
  
  # List fastq files
  lff <- list_fastq_files(
    path_to_fastq,
    paired_end = paired_end,
    pattern = pattern,
    pattern_R1 = pattern_R1,
    pattern_R2 = pattern_R2,
    nb_files = nb_files
  )
  
  # Build fastp commands
  if (paired_end) {
    # Paired-end mode
    cmd <- vector("list", length(lff$fnfs))
    names(cmd) <- lff$fnfs
    
    for (i in seq_along(lff$fnfs)) {
      f_R1 <- lff$fnfs[i]
      f_R2 <- lff$fnrs[i]
      
      base_name <- sub("\\.fastq(\\.gz)?$", "", basename(f_R1))
      
      # Build fastp command
      fastp_cmd <- paste0(
        "fastp",
        " -i ", f_R1,
        " -I ", f_R2,
        " -o ", folder_output, "/", basename(f_R1),
        " -O ", folder_output, "/", basename(f_R2),
        " --thread ", nproc,
        " --qualified_quality_phred ", qualified_quality_phred,
        " --average_qual ", average_qual,
        " --length_required ", length_required,
        " --cut_window_size ", cut_window_size,
        " --cut_mean_quality ", cut_mean_quality
      )
      
      # Add optional flags
      if (detect_adapter) {
        fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --detect_adapter_for_pe")
      }
      if (trim_poly_g) {
        fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --trim_poly_g")
      }
      if (correction) {
        fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --correction")
      }
      if (cut_front) {
        fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --cut_front")
      }
      if (cut_tail) {
        fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --cut_tail")
      }
      
      # Add HTML and JSON reports
      fastp_cmd <- paste0(
        fastp_cmd,
        " --html ", folder_output, "/", base_name, ".fastp.html",
        " --json ", folder_output, "/", base_name, ".fastp.json"
      )
      
      # Add extra fastp arguments if provided
      if (nchar(extra_fastp_args) > 0) {
        fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " ", extra_fastp_args)
      }
      
      cmd[[f_R1]] <- fastp_cmd
    }
    
  } else {
    # Single-end mode
    cmd <- vector("list", length(lff$fnfs))
    names(cmd) <- lff$fnfs
    
    for (f in lff$fnfs) {
      base_name <- sub("\\.fastq(\\.gz)?$", "", basename(f))
      
      # Build fastp command
      fastp_cmd <- paste0(
        "fastp",
        " -i ", f,
        " -o ", folder_output, "/", basename(f),
        " --thread ", nproc,
        " --qualified_quality_phred ", qualified_quality_phred,
        " --average_qual ", average_qual,
        " --length_required ", length_required,
        " --cut_window_size ", cut_window_size,
        " --cut_mean_quality ", cut_mean_quality
      )
      
      # Add optional flags
      if (trim_poly_g) {
        fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --trim_poly_g")
      }
      if (cut_front) {
        fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --cut_front")
      }
      if (cut_tail) {
        fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --cut_tail")
      }
      
      # Add HTML and JSON reports
      fastp_cmd <- paste0(
        fastp_cmd,
        " --html ", folder_output, "/", base_name, ".fastp.html",
        " --json ", folder_output, "/", base_name, ".fastp.json"
      )
      
      # Add extra fastp arguments if provided
      if (nchar(extra_fastp_args) > 0) {
        fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " ", extra_fastp_args)
      }
      
      cmd[[f]] <- fastp_cmd
    }
  }
  
  # Execute commands
  if (cmd_is_run) {
    writeLines(unlist(cmd), paste0(tempdir(), "/script_fastp.sh"))
    system2("bash", paste0(tempdir(), "/script_fastp.sh"))
    message(paste0(
      "Output files are available in the folder ",
      normalizePath(folder_output)
    ))
    unlink(paste0(tempdir(), "/script_fastp.sh"))
  }
  
  # Return file paths if requested
  if (return_file_path) {
    if (paired_end) {
      output_R1 <- file.path(folder_output, basename(lff$fnfs))
      output_R2 <- file.path(folder_output, basename(lff$fnrs))
      return(list("fnfs" = output_R1, "fnrs" = output_R2))
    } else {
      output_files <- file.path(folder_output, basename(lff$fnfs))
      return(list("fnfs" = output_files))
    }
  }
  
  if (!cmd_is_run) {
    return(cmd)
  }

——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
# À intégrer bientôt dans MiscMetabar
——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————

```{r}
devtools::load_all("~/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/MiscMetabar/")
```

## Clean NA values in phyloseq

in clean_pq ? by default to FALSE
Replace "NA" by <NA>
Replace "NA NA" by <NA>


## A slot tax_data ?

The tax_table slot have 2 majors issues:
1. No numeric columns
2. No list columns (for example to complex trait information per OTU/ASV such as biological interactions)

Moreover, it is a good idea to have a class extending phyloseq to add specific slots for storing additional information specific to taxa that do not concerned the taxonomy *per se*.

cf script extend_phyloseq.md

```{r}
setClass("extended_phyloseq",
  contains = "phyloseq",
  slots = list(
    tax_data = "data.frame"
  )
)

# Constructor
extended_phyloseq <- function(otu_table, tax_table, sample_data,
                              tax_data = data.frame()) {
  ps <- phyloseq(otu_table, tax_table, sample_data)
  new("extended_phyloseq", ps,
    tax_data = tax_data
  )
}
```


## vsearch zotu

Le 0.96 correspond à l'autorisation de différence de longeur (pas d'identité)
entre la séquence cible et la séquence requête.

```{sh}
"$program_dir/vsearch/bin/vsearch" --cluster_fast "$user_dir/iupac_out_vsearch_96.fasta" --id 1 --iddef 2 --threads 8 --uc "$user_dir/clusters_100.uc" --centroids "$user_dir/centroids_100.fasta" --query_cov 0.96 --target_cov 0.96
```

-> Correspond exactement à Rsearch::vs_cluster_subseq /!\ vs_cluster_subseq ne permet pas de changer le --query_cov et --target_cov autrement que via le vsearch_options 
-> See also Rsearch::vs_cluster_unoise

# - - - VIZUALISATION - - - - - - - - - - -
——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————

——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
## reshape_ggplot
——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————

https://www.perceptualedge.com/articles/visual_business_intelligence/the_datavis_jitterbug.pdf

```{r}
#' Reshape a ggplot2 object
#'
#' Modify width and font of the titles, subtitle and labs parts of a ggplot2
#' plot.
#'
#' @param plot A ggplot2 object
#' @param width (int, default 60) The width of the Title. Also used to compute default value for
#'   other parts.
#' @param width_subtitle (int, default  = width * fontsize_title /
#'    fontsize_subtitle) The width of the subtitle.
#' @param width_labs (int, default = width * fontsize_title / fontsize_labs)
#'    The width of the x and y labs.
#' @param fontsize_title (int) Font size for the title.
#' @param fontsize_subtitle (int) Font size for the subtitle.
#' @param fontsize_labs (int) Font size for the  x and y labs.
#'
#' @return A ggplot2 object
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
#' if (requireNamespace("stringr")) {
#'   patchwork::wrap_plots(lapply(hill_pq(data_fungi, "Time", vioplot = TRUE),
#'     reshape_ggplot,
#'     width = 45
#'   ))
#'   patchwork::wrap_plots(lapply(ggbetween_pq(data_fungi, "Time"),
#'     reshape_ggplot,
#'     fontsize_subtitle = 5,
#'     fontsize_labs = 10
#'   ))
#' }
reshape_ggplot <- function(plot,
                           width = 60,
                           width_subtitle = width * fontsize_title / fontsize_subtitle,
                           width_labs = width * fontsize_title / fontsize_labs,
                           fontsize_title = 10,
                           fontsize_subtitle = 7,
                           fontsize_labs = 8) {
  if (is.character(plot$labels$title)) {
    plot <- plot +
      labs(title = stringr::str_wrap(plot$labels$title, width = width))
  }

  if (is.character(plot$labels$subtitle)) {
    plot <- plot +
      labs(subtitle = stringr::str_wrap(plot$labels$subtitle, width = width_subtitle))
  }


  plot <- plot + xlab(label = stringr::str_wrap(plot$labels$x, width = width_labs)) +
    ylab(label = stringr::str_wrap(plot$labels$y, width = width_labs)) +
    theme(
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = fontsize_subtitle),
      plot.title = element_text(size = fontsize_title),
      axis.title = element_text(size = fontsize_labs)
    )
  return(plot)
}
```

content.content: 40 ################################################################################ 41 42 ################################################################################ 43 #' Clean phyloseq object by removing empty samples and taxa 44 #' 45 #' @description 46 #' 47 #' 48 #' 49 #' 50 #' In addition, this function check for discrepancy (and rename) between 51 #' (i) taxa names in refseq, taxonomy table and otu_table and between 52 #' (ii) sample names in sam_data and otu_table. 53 #' 54 #' @param physeq (required) a \code{\link[phyloseq]{phyloseq-class}} object obtained 55 #' using the `phyloseq` package. 56 #' @param remove_empty_samples (logical) Do you want to remove samples 57 #' without sequences (this is done after removing empty taxa) 58 #' @param remove_empty_taxa (logical) Do you want to remove taxa 59 #' without sequences (this is done before removing empty samples) 60 #' @param clean_samples_names (logical) Do you want to clean samples names? 61 #' @param silent (logical) If true, no message are printing. 62 #' @param verbose (logical) Additional informations in the message 63 #' the verbose parameter overwrite the silent parameter. 64 #' @param force_taxa_as_columns (logical) If true, if the taxa are rows 65 #' transpose the otu_table and set taxa_are_rows to false 66 #' @param force_taxa_as_rows (logical) If true, if the taxa are columns 67 #' transpose the otu_table and set taxa_are_rows to true 68 #' @param reorder_taxa (logical) if TRUE the otu_table is ordered by the number of 69 #' sequences of taxa (ASV, OTU) in descending order. Default to FALSE. 70 #' @param rename_taxa (logical, default FALSE) if TRUE, taxa (ASV, OTU) are renamed by their position 71 #' in the OTU_table and prefix_taxa_names param (Taxa_1, Taxa_2, ...). 72 #' Default to FALSE. If rename taxa (ASV, OTU) is true, 73 #' the taxa (ASV, OTU) names in verbose information can be misleading. 74 #' @param simplify_taxo (logical) if TRUE, correct the taxonomy_table using the 75 #' `MiscMetabar::simplify_taxo()` function 76 #' @param prefix_taxa_names (default "Taxa_"): the prefix of taxa names (eg. Handles 82 #' both ASCII whitespace and Unicode separators such as NBSP (U+00A0). 83 #' @param tax_remove_all_space (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, replace 84 #' internal whitespace (ASCII or Unicode separator) in `tax_table` values 85 #' with `replace_space_with`. 86 #' @param tax_replace_invisible_chars (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, 87 #' strip invisible / unusual characters (control chars, zero-width space, 88 #' NBSP inside values, ...) from `tax_table` values. See 89 #' [verify_tax_table()]'s `replace_invisible_chars` for the exact pattern. 90 #' @param tax_replace_to_NA (logical or character, default FALSE) If TRUE, 91 #' replace `tax_table` values matching the default [unwanted_tax_patterns] 92 #' with `NA`. A character vector of regex patterns can be supplied to 93 #' override the defaults. 94 #' @param tax_redundant_suffix (logical or character, default FALSE) If TRUE, 95 #' replace redundant `"_sp"` values with `NA` (e.g. `Russula_sp` at Species 96 #' when `Russula` is already at Genus). A character string supplies a 97 #' custom suffix. 98 #' @param tax_replace_space_with (character, default `"_"`) Replacement for 99 #' internal whitespace when `tax_remove_all_space = TRUE`. 100 #' @return A new \code{\link[phyloseq]{phyloseq-class}} object 101 #' @export 102 #' @author Adrien Taudière 103 #' @examples 104 #' clean_pq(data_fungi_mini) 105 #' \donttest{ 106 #' # Trim leading/trailing whitespace in tax_table values 107 #' clean_pq(data_fungi_mini, tax_remove_border_spaces = TRUE) 108 #' # Replace NA-like values (e.g. "unidentified", "NA") with NA 109 #' clean_pq(data_fungi_mini, tax_replace_to_NA = TRUE) 110 #' # Drop redundant "_sp" tips 111 #' clean_pq(data_fungi_mini, tax_redundant_suffix = TRUE) 112 #' } 113 clean_pq <- function( 114 physeq, 115 #'   \item{unknown ranks (PR2 database)}{`"__X+$"`}
#' }
#'
#' @export
#' @seealso [verify_tax_table()]
#' @examples
#' unwanted_tax_patterns
#' # Use with grepl to check a value
#' any(vapply(
#'   unwanted_tax_patterns,
#'   \(pat) grepl(pat, "unclassified"),
#'   logical(1)
#' ))
unwanted_tax_patterns <- c(
  "NA-like (NA, NaN, nan)" = "^[Nn][Aa][Nn]?$",
  "NA-like (N/A, n/a)" = "^[Nn]/[Aa]$",
  "None / none" = "^[Nn]one$",
  "empty string" = "^$",
  "whitespace only" = "^\\s+$",
  "unclassified" = "[Uu]nclassified",
  "unknown" = "[Uu]nknown",
  "unidentified" = "[Uu]nidentified",
  "uncultured" = "[Uu]ncultured",
  "incertae sedis" = "[Ii]ncertae[_\\s]?[Ss]edis",
  "metagenome" = "^[Mm]etagenome$",
  "environmental" = "^[Ee]nvironmental",
  "empty QIIME-style rank" = "^[kpcofgs]__$",
  "unknown species (_sp prefix)" = "^_sp",
  "unknown species (_species prefix)" = "^_species",
  "unknown cluster (_uc prefix, e.g. MMseqs2 assignation)" = "_uc$",
  "unknown ranks (_X, _XX, ... prefix e.g. PR2 database)" = "__X+$"
)

content.text: I have full context now. Let me consult the advisor before writing, since this is a 4-part task with several design decisions.

You've oriented thoroughly and you're calling before writing — good. The highest-value thing I can give you: **the prototypes you just read violate several CRAN rules, so don't port them verbatim.** This package was archived for check-time and is now maintenance-only on CRAN, so that's the tightest constraint and it shapes every example and every new dependency.

## Cross-cutting CRAN constraints (all four)

1. **Suggests packages used in `R/` must be guarded.** `reshape_ggplot` calls `stringr::str_wrap` unconditionally, and the Excel fn will call openxlsx/writexl — all Suggests. Add `if (!requireNamespace("pkg", quietly = TRUE)) cli::cli_abort(...)` at the top of each. Do **not** promote stringr to Imports — it drags in stringi (compiled) and worsens check-time. Guard instead.
2. **Examples must not write outside `tempdir()`.** The `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel` prototype defaults the filename to cwd and its example writes `eui.xlsx` to cwd — that's a CRAN policy violation. Keep the default filename but make any *runnable* example write to `tempdir()` and clean up.
3. **Example run-time.** `data_fungi_mini` has 137 samples. `reshape_ggplot`'s example calls `hill_pq`/`ggbetween_pq` (both loop over samples) → subset to 5 samples, wrap the primary example in `\donttest{}`, and guard `patchwork` (also Suggests) inside the example, not just `stringr`.
4. **Tool/pkg-gated examples:** `fastp` → `\dontrun{}` (needs external binary); excel → `@examplesIf` + `skip_if_not_installed` in the test. Match the existing `cutadapt` pattern (`@examplesIf ...sysname != "windows"`).

These four **block** R CMD check (error/NOTE) — handle them in the first write, not as cleanup.

## Per-function touch list
Each new function = R code + `test-{name}.R` + NEWS bullet (alphabetical by fn name) + `_pkgdown.yml` entry, then `devtools::document()` + `air format` at the end.

- **clean_pq NA-cleanup** — edit `dada_phyloseq.R` only (existing fn + roxygen + NEWS bullet; no pkgdown change).
- **reshape_ggplot** — `plot_functions.R` + `test-reshape_ggplot.R`.
- **phyloseq_to_MDT_excel** — new file + DESCRIPTION Suggests + the rest.
- **fastp** — new `R/fastp.R` + `test-fastp.R` (cleaner than appending to the already-169K `dada_phyloseq.R`, and matches the `test-{name}` convention — but existing convention dumps bioinfo wrappers in `dada_phyloseq.R`; pick one and say which).

## Decisions to settle (verify, don't guess)

- **clean_pq:** single `"NA"` is *already* handled by `tax_replace_to_NA` via `unwanted_tax_patterns` (`^[Nn][Aa][Nn]?$`). The genuinely new capability is the multi-token `"NA NA"`/`"NA NA NA"` case (taxonomy concatenation). Route it through `verify_tax_table` by adding pattern `^NA( NA)*$` rather than building a parallel replacement path. Confirm: case-sensitive literal (roadmap says `"NA"`) vs case-insensitive like the rest — I'd keep it literal to avoid clobbering real data. That pattern also catches single `"NA"` when your new flag is on but `tax_replace_to_NA` is off, which is what you want.
- **Excel lib:** writexl (zero-dependency, lighter for check-time) vs openxlsx (prototype). Darwin Core "compliance checks" are validation *logic*, not cell styling, so writexl suffices. Recommend writexl — mention the swap and proceed; don't spend a second AskUserQuestion on it.
- **reshape_ggplot robustness:** the prototype guards `title`/`subtitle` for NULL but not `x`/`y`. In ggplot2 3.5+ (your Depends) an unset axis label isn't a plain string — `str_wrap()` on it can error. Guard x/y the same way and add a test on a plot with no axis labels.

## One scope question that doesn't block
`phyloseq_to_MDT_excel` — the roadmap asks for "Darwin Core DNA-derived-data compliance checks." Decide now: basic required-fields warning in this pass, or deferred follow-up. Either is fine, but don't silently drop it — if deferring, leave a NEWS/roadmap note.

cat >> R/plot_functions.R << 'ENDOFFUNC' #' Reshape a ggplot2 object by wrapping its text elements #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' Auto-wrap (and optionally resize the font of) the title, subtitle and axis #' labels of a ggplot2 plot using [stringr::str_wrap()]. Useful to tidy up long #' titles produced automatically by other `MiscMetabar` plotting functions, in #' particular before combining several plots with `patchwork`. #' #' @param plot (required) A ggplot2 object. #' @param width (int, default 60) The wrapping width (in characters) of the #' title. Also used to compute the default value of the other widths. #' @param width_subtitle (int, default `width * fontsize_title / #' fontsize_subtitle`) The wrapping width of the subtitle. #' @param width_labs (int, default `width * fontsize_title / fontsize_labs`) #' The wrapping width of the x and y axis labels. #' @param fontsize_title (int, default 10) Font size for the title. #' @param fontsize_subtitle (int, default 7) Font size for the subtitle. #' @param fontsize_labs (int, default 8) Font size for the x and y axis labels. #' #' @return A ggplot2 object with wrapped/resized text elements. #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' if (requireNamespace("stringr") && requireNamespace("patchwork")) { #' df_mini <- prune_samples( #' sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], #' data_fungi_mini #' ) #' patchwork::wrap_plots(lapply( #' hill_pq(df_mini, "Height", vioplot = TRUE), #' reshape_ggplot, #' width = 45 #' )) #' } #' } reshape_ggplot <- function( plot, width = 60, width_subtitle = width * fontsize_title / fontsize_subtitle, width_labs = width * fontsize_title / fontsize_labs, fontsize_title = 10, fontsize_subtitle = 7, fontsize_labs = 8 ) { if (!requireNamespace("stringr", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg stringr} is required for {.fn reshape_ggplot}. Please install it." ) } if (is.character(plot$labels$title)) { plot <- plot + labs(title = stringr::str_wrap(plot$labels$title, width = width)) } if (is.character(plot$labels$subtitle)) { plot <- plot + labs( subtitle = stringr::str_wrap( plot$labels$subtitle, width = width_subtitle ) ) } if (is.character(plot$labels$x)) { plot <- plot + xlab(label = stringr::str_wrap(plot$labels$x, width = width_labs)) } if (is.character(plot$labels$y)) { plot <- plot + ylab(label = stringr::str_wrap(plot$labels$y, width = width_labs)) } plot <- plot + theme( plot.subtitle = element_text(size = fontsize_subtitle), plot.title = element_text(size = fontsize_title), axis.title = element_text(size = fontsize_labs) ) return(plot) } ################################################################################ ENDOFFUNC echo "appended"

content.text: The dada_phyloseq lints are pre-existing (in the `clean_samples_names` block, untouched by me) — noise. Now `phyloseq_to_MDT_excel()` as a new file using `writexl` (lighter than openxlsx), with a basic Darwin Core check:

input.content: ################################################################################ #' Export a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF MDT submission #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' Write the OTU table, the sample data, the taxonomy table and (if present) the #' reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a single multi-sheet `.xlsx` #' file, formatted for submission to the GBIF #' [Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT)](https://mdt.gbif-uat.org/). #' #' Each sheet gets a leading `id` column holding the row identifiers (taxa or #' samples names). The reference sequences, when available, are appended as a #' `DNA_sequence` column of the taxonomy sheet (Darwin Core DNA-derived-data #' extension term). #' #' When `check_dwc = TRUE` (the default), a lightweight Darwin Core compliance #' check warns about recommended sample-level terms that are missing from #' `sample_data` (`decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`, `eventDate`). This is #' a non-blocking helper, not a full validation of the GBIF MDT template. #' #' @param physeq (required) a \code{\link[phyloseq]{phyloseq-class}} object. #' @param filename (character, default `"Phyloseq_Tables.xlsx"`) Path of the #' output `.xlsx` file. #' @param check_dwc (logical, default TRUE) If TRUE, warn about recommended #' Darwin Core sample-level terms missing from `sample_data`. #' #' @return Invisibly returns the path to the written file (`filename`). #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details #' This function requires the \pkg{writexl} package. See the GBIF MDT #' [user guide](https://docs.gbif-uat.org/mdt-user-guide/en/) for the expected #' input format. #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' if (requireNamespace("writexl")) { #' out <- file.path(tempdir(), "data_fungi_mini_MDT.xlsx") #' phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(clean_pq(data_fungi_mini), filename = out) #' file.exists(out) #' unlink(out) #' } #' } phyloseq_to_MDT_excel <- function( physeq, filename = "Phyloseq_Tables.xlsx", check_dwc = TRUE ) { if (!requireNamespace("writexl", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg writexl} is required for {.fn phyloseq_to_MDT_excel}. Please install it." ) } verify_pq(physeq) # OTU table: samples in rows, taxa in columns if (taxa_are_rows(physeq)) { otu_table_df <- as.data.frame(t(as(physeq@otu_table, "matrix"))) } else { otu_table_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@otu_table, "matrix")) } otu_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(otu_table_df), otu_table_df) # Sample data sample_sheet <- NULL if (!is.null(physeq@sam_data)) { sample_data_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@sam_data, "data.frame")) sample_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(sample_data_df), sample_data_df) if (check_dwc) { recommended <- c("decimalLatitude", "decimalLongitude", "eventDate") missing_terms <- setdiff(recommended, colnames(sample_data_df)) if (length(missing_terms) > 0) { cli::cli_warn(c( "!" = "Recommended Darwin Core sample term{?s} missing from {.field sample_data}: {.val {missing_terms}}.", "i" = "Add {?it/them} before GBIF MDT submission if available." )) } } } # Taxonomy table (+ refseq as DNA_sequence) taxonomy_sheet <- NULL if (!is.null(physeq@tax_table)) { tax_table_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@tax_table, "matrix")) if (!is.null(physeq@refseq)) { tax_table_df[["DNA_sequence"]] <- as.character(physeq@refseq) } taxonomy_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(tax_table_df), tax_table_df) } sheets <- list(OTU_table = otu_sheet) if (!is.null(sample_sheet)) { sheets[["Samples"]] <- sample_sheet } if (!is.null(taxonomy_sheet)) { sheets[["Taxonomy"]] <- taxonomy_sheet } writexl::write_xlsx(sheets, path = filename) message("Excel file written to ", normalizePath(filename)) invisible(filename) } ################################################################################

content.text: Now the `fastp()` wrapper in a new `R/fastp.R`, with a tool-presence helper and lifecycle badge:

input.content: ################################################################################ #' Fastp: adapter detection, quality control and preprocessing of FASTQ files #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' This function is a wrapper for the #' [fastp](https://github.com/OpenGene/fastp) software to perform quality #' control and preprocessing of FASTQ files (paired-end or single-end). #' #' To install fastp on linux: #' #' ```bash #' wget http://opengene.org/fastp/fastp #' chmod a+x ./fastp #' sudo mv fastp /usr/local/bin/ #' ``` #' #' For other OS or specific installation instructions, please refer to the #' official fastp GitHub repository: . #' #' @param path_to_fastq (required) Path to the directory containing FASTQ files. #' See [list_fastq_files()] for help. #' @inheritParams list_fastq_files #' @param folder_output Name of the output folder (default: "fastp_output"). #' @param nproc Number of threads to use (default: 1). #' @param paired_end Logical, whether files are paired-end (default: TRUE). #' @param cmd_is_run Logical, whether to run the command (default: TRUE). If #' FALSE, the only effect of the function is to return a list of commands to #' run manually in a terminal. #' @param return_file_path Logical, whether to return output file paths #' (default: FALSE). Useful in `targets` workflows. #' @param qualified_quality_phred Minimum base quality (default: 20). #' @param average_qual Minimum average read quality (default: 20). #' @param length_required Minimum read length after trimming (default: 50). #' @param detect_adapter Logical, enable automatic adapter detection for #' paired-end reads (default: TRUE). #' @param trim_poly_g Logical, trim poly-G tails (default: TRUE). #' @param correction Logical, correct mismatches in overlapped paired-end reads #' (default: TRUE). #' @param cut_front Logical, trim low quality bases from 5' end (default: TRUE). #' @param cut_tail Logical, trim low quality bases from 3' end (default: TRUE). #' @param cut_window_size Window size for sliding quality filter (default: 4). #' @param cut_mean_quality Minimum mean quality for trimming window #' (default: 20). #' @param extra_fastp_args Additional fastp arguments as a character string #' (default: ""). #' #' @return If `cmd_is_run = FALSE`, a named list of fastp commands. If #' `return_file_path = TRUE`, a list of output file paths. Otherwise `NULL` #' invisibly, after running fastp. #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @seealso [is_fastp_installed()], [list_fastq_files()] #' @details #' This function is a wrapper for the fastp software, please cite fastp #' (\doi{10.1093/bioinformatics/bty560}) if you use this function. #' #' @examples #' \dontrun{ #' # Paired-end example #' fastp( #' path_to_fastq = system.file("extdata", package = "MiscMetabar"), #' folder_output = file.path(tempdir(), "qc_data"), #' nproc = 8, #' paired_end = TRUE #' ) #' #' # Single-end example with extra options #' fastp( #' path_to_fastq = "raw_data/", #' folder_output = file.path(tempdir(), "qc_data"), #' nproc = 8, #' paired_end = FALSE, #' extra_fastp_args = "--merge --n_base_limit 5 --dedup" #' ) #' #' # Only build the commands, do not run them #' fastp( #' path_to_fastq = system.file("extdata", package = "MiscMetabar"), #' cmd_is_run = FALSE #' ) #' } fastp <- function( path_to_fastq, folder_output = "fastp_output", nproc = 1, pattern = "fastq.gz", pattern_R1 = "_R1", pattern_R2 = "_R2", nb_files = Inf, paired_end = TRUE, cmd_is_run = TRUE, return_file_path = FALSE, qualified_quality_phred = 20, average_qual = 20, length_required = 50, detect_adapter = TRUE, trim_poly_g = TRUE, correction = TRUE, cut_front = TRUE, cut_tail = TRUE, cut_window_size = 4, cut_mean_quality = 20, extra_fastp_args = "" ) { if (!dir.exists(folder_output)) { dir.create(folder_output, recursive = TRUE) } lff <- list_fastq_files( path_to_fastq, paired_end = paired_end, pattern = pattern, pattern_R1 = pattern_R1, pattern_R2 = pattern_R2, nb_files = nb_files ) cmd <- vector("list", length(lff$fnfs)) # pre-allocated for performance names(cmd) <- lff$fnfs for (i in seq_along(lff$fnfs)) { f_R1 <- lff$fnfs[i] base_name <- sub("\\.fastq(\\.gz)?$", "", basename(f_R1)) fastp_cmd <- paste0( "fastp", " -i ", f_R1, " -o ", folder_output, "/", basename(f_R1), " --thread ", nproc, " --qualified_quality_phred ", qualified_quality_phred, " --average_qual ", average_qual, " --length_required ", length_required, " --cut_window_size ", cut_window_size, " --cut_mean_quality ", cut_mean_quality ) if (paired_end) { f_R2 <- lff$fnrs[i] fastp_cmd <- paste0( fastp_cmd, " -I ", f_R2, " -O ", folder_output, "/", basename(f_R2) ) if (detect_adapter) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --detect_adapter_for_pe") } if (correction) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --correction") } } if (trim_poly_g) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --trim_poly_g") } if (cut_front) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --cut_front") } if (cut_tail) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --cut_tail") } fastp_cmd <- paste0( fastp_cmd, " --html ", folder_output, "/", base_name, ".fastp.html", " --json ", folder_output, "/", base_name, ".fastp.json" ) if (nchar(extra_fastp_args) > 0) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " ", extra_fastp_args) } cmd[[f_R1]] <- fastp_cmd } if (cmd_is_run) { script_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "script_fastp.sh") writeLines(unlist(cmd), script_path) system2("bash", script_path) message( "Output files are available in the folder ", normalizePath(folder_output) ) unlink(script_path) } else { return(cmd) } if (return_file_path) { output_R1 <- file.path(folder_output, basename(lff$fnfs)) if (paired_end) { output_R2 <- file.path(folder_output, basename(lff$fnrs)) return(list("fnfs" = output_R1, "fnrs" = output_R2)) } else { return(list("fnfs" = output_R1)) } } invisible(NULL) } ################################################################################ ################################################################################ #' Test if fastp is installed. #' #' @description #' #' #' #' Useful for testthat and examples compilation for R CMD CHECK and #' test coverage. #' #' @param path (default: fastp) Path to fastp. #' @export #' @return A logical that say if fastp is installed. #' @seealso [fastp()] #' @examples #' MiscMetabar::is_fastp_installed() #' @author Adrien Taudière is_fastp_installed <- function(path = "fastp") { return( !inherits( try( system2(path, "--version", stdout = TRUE, stderr = TRUE), silent = TRUE ), "try-error" ) ) } ################################################################################

  role = c("aut", "cre", "cph"), comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0003-1088-1182"))
Description: Facilitate the description, transformation, exploration, and reproducibility of metabarcoding analyses. 'MiscMetabar' is mainly built on top of the 'phyloseq', 'dada2' and 'targets' 'R' packages. It helps to build reproducible and robust bioinformatics pipelines in 'R'. 'MiscMetabar' makes ecological analysis of alpha and beta-diversity easier, more reproducible and more powerful by integrating a large number of tools. Important features are described in Taudière A. base_name <- sub("\\.fastq(\\.gz)?$", "", basename(f_R1)) fastp_cmd <- paste0( "fastp", " -i ", f_R1, " -o ", folder_output, "/", basename(f_R1), " --thread ", nproc, " --qualified_quality_phred ", qualified_quality_phred, " --average_qual ", average_qual, " --length_required ", length_required, " --cut_window_size ", cut_window_size, " --cut_mean_quality ", cut_mean_quality ) if (paired_end) { f_R2 <- lff$fnrs[i] fastp_cmd <- paste0( fastp_cmd, " -I ", f_R2, " -O ", folder_output, "/", basename(f_R2) ) if (detect_adapter) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --detect_adapter_for_pe") } if (correction) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --correction") } } if (trim_poly_g) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --trim_poly_g") } if (cut_front) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --cut_front") } if (cut_tail) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " --cut_tail") } fastp_cmd <- paste0( fastp_cmd, " --html ", folder_output, "/", base_name, ".fastp.html", " --json ", folder_output, "/", base_name, ".fastp.json" ) if (nchar(extra_fastp_args) > 0) { fastp_cmd <- paste0(fastp_cmd, " ", extra_fastp_args) } cmd[[f_R1]] <- fastp_cmd } if (cmd_is_run) { script_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "script_fastp.sh") writeLines(unlist(cmd), script_path) system2("bash", script_path) message( "Output files are available in the folder ", normalizePath(folder_output) ) unlink(script_path) } else { return(cmd) } if (return_file_path) { output_R1 <- file.path(folder_output, basename(lff$fnfs)) if (paired_end) { output_R2 <- file.path(folder_output, basename(lff$fnrs)) return(list("fnfs" = output_R1, "fnrs" = output_R2)) } else { return(list("fnfs" = output_R1)) } } invisible(NULL) } ################################################################################ ################################################################################ #' Test if fastp is installed. #' #' @description #' #' #' #' Useful for testthat and examples compilation for R CMD CHECK and #' test coverage. #' #' @param path (default: fastp) Path to fastp. #' @export #' @return A logical that say if fastp is installed. #' @seealso [fastp()] #' @examples #' MiscMetabar::is_fastp_installed() #' @author Adrien Taudière is_fastp_installed <- function(path = "fastp") { return( !inherits( try( system2(path, "--version", stdout = TRUE, stderr = TRUE), silent = TRUE ), "try-error" ) ) } ################################################################################

content.text: Now I'll add a test for the NA-string cleanup:

input.old_string:
test_that("clean_pq tax_redundant_suffix accepts a custom suffix", {
  pq <- data_fungi_mini
  pq@tax_table[1, "Genus"] <- "Russula"
  pq@tax_table[1, "Species"] <- "Russula_var"
  res <- suppressMessages(clean_pq(pq, tax_redundant_suffix = "_var"))
  expect_true(is.na(res@tax_table[1, "Species"]))
})
input.new_string:
test_that("clean_pq tax_redundant_suffix accepts a custom suffix", {
  pq <- data_fungi_mini
  pq@tax_table[1, "Genus"] <- "Russula"
  pq@tax_table[1, "Species"] <- "Russula_var"
  res <- suppressMessages(clean_pq(pq, tax_redundant_suffix = "_var"))
  expect_true(is.na(res@tax_table[1, "Species"]))
})

test_that("clean_pq tax_replace_NA_string replaces 'NA' and 'NA NA' with <NA>", {
  pq <- data_fungi_mini
  pq@tax_table[1, "Genus"] <- "NA"
  pq@tax_table[2, "Species"] <- "NA NA"
  pq@tax_table[3, "Species"] <- "NA NA NA"
  res <- suppressMessages(clean_pq(pq, tax_replace_NA_string = TRUE))
  expect_true(is.na(res@tax_table[1, "Genus"]))
  expect_true(is.na(res@tax_table[2, "Species"]))
  expect_true(is.na(res@tax_table[3, "Species"]))
})

test_that("clean_pq tax_replace_NA_string is case-sensitive and keeps real data", {
  pq <- data_fungi_mini
  pq@tax_table[1, "Genus"] <- "Navas"
  pq@tax_table[2, "Genus"] <- "na"
  res <- suppressMessages(clean_pq(pq, tax_replace_NA_string = TRUE))
  expect_identical(as.character(res@tax_table[1, "Genus"]), "Navas")
  expect_identical(as.character(res@tax_table[2, "Genus"]), "na")
})

test_that("fastp builds paired-end commands without running them", {
  skip_on_cran()
  cmd <- fastp(

content.text: Now the excel and reshape tests:

input.content:
test_that("phyloseq_to_MDT_excel writes a multi-sheet xlsx file", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("writexl")
  out <- file.path(tempdir(), "mdt_test.xlsx")
  unlink(out)
  res <- suppressMessages(
    phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(clean_pq(data_fungi_mini), filename = out)
  )
  expect_identical(res, out)
  expect_true(file.exists(out))
  expect_gt(file.info(out)$size, 0)
  unlink(out)
})

test_that("phyloseq_to_MDT_excel warns about missing Darwin Core terms", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("writexl")
  out <- file.path(tempdir(), "mdt_dwc.xlsx")
  unlink(out)
  expect_warning(
    suppressMessages(
      phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(clean_pq(data_fungi_mini), filename = out)
    ),
    "decimalLatitude"
  )
  unlink(out)
})

test_that("phyloseq_to_MDT_excel check_dwc = FALSE is silent about DwC terms", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("writexl")
  out <- file.path(tempdir(), "mdt_nodwc.xlsx")
  unlink(out)
  expect_no_warning(
    suppressMessages(
      phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(
        clean_pq(data_fungi_mini),
        filename = out,
        check_dwc = FALSE
      )
    )
  )
  unlink(out)
})

test_that("reshape_ggplot wraps long titles and returns a ggplot", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("stringr")
  long_title <- paste(rep("word", 30), collapse = " ")
  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(
    data.frame(x = 1:3, y = 1:3),
    ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = y)
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_point() +
    ggplot2::labs(title = long_title, subtitle = long_title)
  res <- reshape_ggplot(p, width = 20)
  expect_s3_class(res, "ggplot")
  expect_match(res$labels$title, "\n")
  expect_match(res$labels$subtitle, "\n")
})

test_that("reshape_ggplot does not error when axis labels are missing", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("stringr")
  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(
    data.frame(x = 1:3, y = 1:3),
    ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = y)
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_point() +
    ggplot2::labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = NULL)
  expect_s3_class(reshape_ggplot(p), "ggplot")
})

1	# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000
2	* Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.

7	  extra examples `\dontrun{}` (kept for documentation,

17	  positive-subset (`x <- x[x > 0]`) once per Hill order. The loop is

file.content:
# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000
* Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.
* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.

# MiscMetabar 0.16.8
* Further check-time reductions for CRAN compliance:
  extra examples `\dontrun{}` (kept for documentation,
  not run during checks). 
* Added file-level `skip_on_cran()` to some heavy test files.
* Example speed-ups for some functions.
* Fix several bugs when using Windows paths by quoting system call arguments with `shQuote()`


# MiscMetabar 0.16.6 
* `verify_tax_table()` is now ~10× faster on full-size taxonomy tables.
* `divent_hill_matrix_pq()` no longer recomputes the per-sample
  positive-subset (`x <- x[x > 0]`) once per Hill order. The loop is
  now sample-outer / q-inner, so each row is sliced once. Numeric
  output is bitwise-identical. Speeds up every Hill-diversity
  computation in the package: `hill_pq()`, `hill_bar_pq()`,
  `hill_tuckey_pq()`, `profile_hill_pq()`, `psmelt_samples_pq()`,
  `plot_refseq_extremity_pq()`, and the `*_rarperm_pq` family.
* `circle_pq()` replaces a nested
  `pbapply(., 2, pbtapply(., group, sum))` over the OTU table with
  two `rowsum()` calls. On `data_fungi` (1420 taxa × 185 samples)
  the example dropped from ~18 s to ~1.8 s (≈ 10× faster). Output
  unchanged.
* `format2dada2(fasta_db = …)`, `hill_acc_pq(type = "sample")`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()` are also faster.
* New pkgdown article: `vignettes/articles/timing.Rmd` documents
  wall-clock cost of the main functions on `data_fungi` and

  bslib:
    bg: "#faefd1"
    fg: "#003f5f"
    primary: "#b96821"
    secondary: "#c8a734"
  includes:
    in_header: |
      <script data-goatcounter="https://miscmetabar.goatcounter.com/count"
              async src="//gc.zgo.at/count.js"></script>
navbar:
  bg: secondary
  components:
   articles:
    text: Articles
    menu:
    - text: Tutorials
    - text: Import/export and track
      href: articles/import_export_track.html
    - text: Explore phyloseq data
      href: articles/explore_data.html
    - text: Filter taxa and samples
      href: articles/filter.html
    - text: Transformation and normalisation
      href: articles/normalization.html
    - text: Reclustering
      href: articles/Reclustering.html
    - text: Tree building and visualization
      href: articles/tree_visualization.html
    - text: Function timing
      href: articles/timing.html
    - text: -------
    - text: Diversity analysis
    - text: Alpha-diversity
      href: articles/alpha-div.html
    - text: Beta-diversity
      href: articles/beta-div.html
    - text: -------
    - text: Bioinformatics actions
    - text: Fastq quality check
      href: articles/fastq_quality_check.html
    - text: -------
    - text: Examples with published dataset
    - text: Tengeler dataset
      href: articles/tengeler.html
    - text: -------
    - text: "R ecosystem for metabarcoding"
    - text: "Metabarcoding with R"
      href: articles/states_of_fields_in_R.html
    - text: -------
    - text: "For developpers"
    - text: Rules of code
      href: articles/Rules.html
    - text: Report pkgnet
      href: articles/pkgnet-report.html

content.text: Now let me check whether `writexl` is installed, regenerate docs, and format:

input.command:
Rscript -e 'cat("writexl:", requireNamespace("writexl", quietly=TRUE), "
"); cat("stringr:", requireNamespace("stringr", quietly=TRUE), "
"); cat("patchwork:", requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly=TRUE), "
")'

content.content:
writexl: FALSE 
stringr: TRUE 
patchwork: TRUE

toolUseResult.stdout:
writexl: FALSE 
stringr: TRUE 
patchwork: TRUE

content.text: writexl isn't installed — I'll install it (lightweight, zero-compile) so I can verify the function, then document and format.

input.command: Rscript -e 'install.packages("writexl", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org", quiet=TRUE); cat("installed:", requireNamespace("writexl", quietly=TRUE), "\n")'

content.content: installed: TRUE

toolUseResult.stdout: installed: TRUE

content.text: All four new exports are present. Now let me run `air format` and then the tests:

input.command: air format R/fastp.R R/phyloseq_to_MDT_excel.R R/dada_phyloseq.R R/plot_functions.R tests/testthat/test_clean_pq.R tests/testthat/test_fastp.R tests/testthat/test_phyloseq_to_MDT_excel.R tests/testthat/test_reshape_ggplot.R && echo "FORMAT_OK"

content.content: FORMAT_OK

toolUseResult.stdout: FORMAT_OK

content.text: Now run the four test files:

input.command:
Rscript -e '
suppressMessages(devtools::load_all("."))
library(testthat)
for (f in c("test_clean_pq", "test_fastp", "test_phyloseq_to_MDT_excel", "test_reshape_ggplot")) {
  cat("\n=====", f, "=====\n")
  test_file(file.path("tests/testthat", paste0(f, ".R")))
}
'

content.text: The `skip_on_cran()` fired because `NOT_CRAN` isn't set in this invocation. Let me set it to actually exercise the tests:

input.command:
NOT_CRAN=true Rscript -e '
suppressMessages(devtools::load_all("."))
library(testthat)
for (f in c("test_clean_pq", "test_fastp", "test_phyloseq_to_MDT_excel", "test_reshape_ggplot")) {
  cat("\n=====", f, "=====\n")
  res <- test_file(file.path("tests/testthat", paste0(f, ".R")), reporter = "summary")
}
'

content.content: The file /home/adrien/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar/R/phyloseq_to_MDT_excel.R has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

toolUseResult.originalFile: ################################################################################ #' Export a phyloseq object to a multi-sheet Excel file for GBIF MDT submission #' #' @description #' #' #' #' #' Write the OTU table, the sample data, the taxonomy table and (if present) the #' reference sequences of a phyloseq object to a single multi-sheet `.xlsx` #' file, formatted for submission to the GBIF #' [Metabarcoding Data Toolkit (MDT)](https://mdt.gbif-uat.org/). #' #' Each sheet gets a leading `id` column holding the row identifiers (taxa or #' samples names). The reference sequences, when available, are appended as a #' `DNA_sequence` column of the taxonomy sheet (Darwin Core DNA-derived-data #' extension term). #' #' When `check_dwc = TRUE` (the default), a lightweight Darwin Core compliance #' check warns about recommended sample-level terms that are missing from #' `sample_data` (`decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`, `eventDate`). This is #' a non-blocking helper, not a full validation of the GBIF MDT template. #' #' @param physeq (required) a \code{\link[phyloseq]{phyloseq-class}} object. #' @param filename (character, default `"Phyloseq_Tables.xlsx"`) Path of the #' output `.xlsx` file. #' @param check_dwc (logical, default TRUE) If TRUE, warn about recommended #' Darwin Core sample-level terms missing from `sample_data`. #' #' @return Invisibly returns the path to the written file (`filename`). #' @export #' @author Adrien Taudière #' @details #' This function requires the \pkg{writexl} package. See the GBIF MDT #' [user guide](https://docs.gbif-uat.org/mdt-user-guide/en/) for the expected #' input format. #' #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' if (requireNamespace("writexl")) { #' out <- file.path(tempdir(), "data_fungi_mini_MDT.xlsx") #' phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(clean_pq(data_fungi_mini), filename = out) #' file.exists(out) #' unlink(out) #' } #' } phyloseq_to_MDT_excel <- function( physeq, filename = "Phyloseq_Tables.xlsx", check_dwc = TRUE ) { if (!requireNamespace("writexl", quietly = TRUE)) { cli::cli_abort( "Package {.pkg writexl} is required for {.fn phyloseq_to_MDT_excel}. Please install it." ) } verify_pq(physeq) # OTU table: samples in rows, taxa in columns if (taxa_are_rows(physeq)) { otu_table_df <- as.data.frame(t(as(physeq@otu_table, "matrix"))) } else { otu_table_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@otu_table, "matrix")) } otu_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(otu_table_df), otu_table_df) # Sample data sample_sheet <- NULL if (!is.null(physeq@sam_data)) { sample_data_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@sam_data, "data.frame")) sample_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(sample_data_df), sample_data_df) if (check_dwc) { recommended <- c("decimalLatitude", "decimalLongitude", "eventDate") missing_terms <- setdiff(recommended, colnames(sample_data_df)) if (length(missing_terms) > 0) { cli::cli_warn(c( "!" = "Recommended Darwin Core sample term{?s} missing from {.field sample_data}: {.val {missing_terms}}.", "i" = "Add {?it/them} before GBIF MDT submission if available." )) } } } # Taxonomy table (+ refseq as DNA_sequence) taxonomy_sheet <- NULL if (!is.null(physeq@tax_table)) { tax_table_df <- as.data.frame(as(physeq@tax_table, "matrix")) if (!is.null(physeq@refseq)) { tax_table_df[["DNA_sequence"]] <- as.character(physeq@refseq) } taxonomy_sheet <- cbind(id = rownames(tax_table_df), tax_table_df) } sheets <- list(OTU_table = otu_sheet) if (!is.null(sample_sheet)) { sheets[["Samples"]] <- sample_sheet } if (!is.null(taxonomy_sheet)) { sheets[["Taxonomy"]] <- taxonomy_sheet } writexl::write_xlsx(sheets, path = filename) message("Excel file written to ", normalizePath(filename)) invisible(filename) } ################################################################################

lines: "!" = "Recommended Darwin Core sample term{?s} missing from {.field sample_data}: {.val {missing_terms}}.",
-          "i" = "Add {?it/them} before GBIF MDT submission if available."
+          "i" = "Add them before GBIF MDT submission if available."

test_that("phyloseq_to_MDT_excel writes a multi-sheet xlsx file", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("writexl")
  out <- file.path(tempdir(), "mdt_test.xlsx")
  unlink(out)
  res <- suppressMessages(
    phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(clean_pq(data_fungi_mini), filename = out)
  )
  expect_identical(res, out)
  expect_true(file.exists(out))
  expect_gt(file.info(out)$size, 0)
  unlink(out)
})

test_that("phyloseq_to_MDT_excel warns about missing Darwin Core terms", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("writexl")
  out <- file.path(tempdir(), "mdt_dwc.xlsx")
  unlink(out)
  expect_warning(
    suppressMessages(
      phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(clean_pq(data_fungi_mini), filename = out)
    ),
    "decimalLatitude"
  )
  unlink(out)
})

test_that("phyloseq_to_MDT_excel check_dwc = FALSE is silent about DwC terms", {
  skip_on_cran()
  skip_if_not_installed("writexl")
  out <- file.path(tempdir(), "mdt_nodwc.xlsx")
  unlink(out)
  expect_no_warning(
    suppressMessages(
      phyloseq_to_MDT_excel(
        clean_pq(data_fungi_mini),
        filename = out,
        check_dwc = FALSE
      )
    )
  )
  unlink(out)
})
